name: Multi-hour refactor that exceeds agent context window
description: User asks to refactor a 150-file legacy Ruby on Rails codebase to extract a billing module into a standalone service. This can't fit in one agent's context. Needs planning, chunking, checkpointing, and verification at each stage.
task: A user says: 'Extract the billing module out of our 150-file Rails monolith into a standalone service.' This is too big for one context window. Design the agent orchestration: (1) the phase breakdown (planning, extraction, testing, integration), (2) how to maintain state across phases when context gets compacted, (3) the checkpoint/resume format, (4) how to verify each phase before moving on, (5) how to handle a sub-task that discovers it needs to edit more than its assigned files, (6) how to know when to stop and ask the user a question vs forge ahead. Provide the orchestration protocol, not the actual Rails code.
expected_outcomes: Explicit phase breakdown with gates between phases
State persistence approach (file-based checkpoint, task list, etc.)
Verification step for each phase (tests pass, grep shows no stray references)
Sub-task escalation protocol when scope creeps
Clear criteria for when to ask the user
Handles context compaction across long sessions
